pages.title: Protocol Overview
pages.description: This section outlines the design, contract architecture, staking & reward mechanisms, incentive alignment and governance model of the on-chain Livepeer Protocol that governs how work is coordinated, secured and rewarded and how upgrades are proposed and implemented.

headings.text: <Icon icon="map-location" size={22} color="var(--accent)" /> {" "} Protocol Section Map

pages.title: Core Mechanisms and Functions
pages.description: The Livepeer protocol secures a decentralised marketplace for verifiable media and AI compute through a tightly coupled set of economic and cryptographic systems. These mechanisms include LPT bonding, dynamic inflation, staking and delegation, slashing conditions, and rewards distribution - all executed through Ethereum-based smart contracts on Arbitrum. This page details each core mechanism, how it interacts with others, and why these design decisions underpin Livepeer’s scalable, permissionless infrastructure.

pages.title: Livepeer Token
pages.description: Livepeer Token (LPT) is the native proof-of-stake token of the Livepeer network. It is the key to participating in the protocol: to operate or delegate in the transcoding network, to earn rewards, and to vote on governance proposals. This page details the technical attributes, token supply dynamics, inflation model, and contract integrations that define LPT’s role.

pages.title: Livepeer Governance Model
pages.description: This page describes the on-chain governance model which provides a permissionless, transparent, and community-driven framework for upgrading and governing the Livepeer protocol. It touches on the key actors, processes and mechanisms that drive coordination and growth of the Livepeer ecosystem.

pages.title: Livepeer Treasury
pages.description: This page outlines how the treasury accumulates funds, how disbursements are authorised, and how its role is evolving across the protocol’s roadmap.

pages.title: Technical Overview
pages.description: A technical overview of the Livepeer protocol.

pages.title: Livepeer Network Overview
pages.description: An overview of the Livepeer network and its components.

pages.title: Actors Overview
pages.description: Who participates in Livepeer, what each role does, and how roles interact.

pages.title: Livepeer Job Lifecycle
pages.description: This page describes the end-to-end “compute job” as a state machine. Since Livepeer’s compute is segment-oriented, the lifecycle is modelled at the level of a stream session and per-segment processing, with payment settlement occurring continuously via tickets and periodically via reward calls.

pages.title: Livepeer Marketplace
pages.description: How the Livepeer Network marketplace matches demand and supply for real-time media compute: transcoding and AI inference, including routing, pricing, and settlement.

pages.title: Network Technical Architecture
pages.description: Technical stack of the Livepeer Network: node types, gateway infrastructure, APIs, CLI, SDKs, and monitoring. How orchestrators, gateways, and workers fit together.

pages.title: Network Interfaces

pages.title: Blockchain Contracts

pages.title: Gateways Vs. Orchestrators: What’s the Difference?
pages.description: Livepeer uses two core node types—**Gateways** and **Orchestrators**—that work together to provide real-time AI video compute at scale. Although closely connected, they serve entirely different purposes. This page breaks down how they differ and why both roles matter for a decentralized compute marketplace.

4. JSX Files Cannot Import Other JSX Files
9. JSX Comments Don't Prevent MDX Parsing
Correct Pattern: Import in MDX, Use in Component
